content.stem: You see this announcement in an English-language magazine. Write your article. Write 140–190 words.
content.prompt:
ARTICLES WANTED!

THE MOST USEFUL SKILL I HAVE LEARNED

Tell us about a skill you have learned that has been useful to you.
How did you learn it?
Why has it been so useful in your life?

Write us an article answering these questions.
The best articles will be published in next month's issue!
content.sample_answer:
The Most Useful Skill I Have Learned

Of all the skills I have acquired over the years, I believe that learning to cook has been the most valuable. It is a skill that I use every single day, and it has changed my life in many positive ways.

I first learned to cook when I was fourteen. My grandmother, who was an excellent cook, spent a whole summer teaching me her favourite recipes. At first, I could barely boil an egg, but gradually I became more confident. I started experimenting with different ingredients and soon discovered that cooking could be genuinely creative and enjoyable.

This skill has been incredibly useful for several reasons. Most importantly, it has allowed me to eat healthy, home-made meals instead of relying on fast food. It has also saved me a considerable amount of money. Moreover, cooking for friends and family has become one of my greatest pleasures and has helped me strengthen my relationships.

I would highly recommend that everyone learns to cook — it is a skill that will serve you well throughout your life.

explanation_zh: 中文解析：FCE 写作 Part 2 是选做题，这里是杂志文章。要求 140-190 词，回答提出的所有问题。文章需要有吸引人的标题，清晰的段落结构，以及生动的个人经历描述。使用高级词汇和复杂句式，如 "Of all the skills I have acquired"、"Moreover"、"It is a skill that will serve you well"。
explanation_en: English explanation: FCE Writing Part 2 offers a choice of tasks — this is a magazine article (140-190 words). Answer all questions posed. Your article needs an engaging title, clear paragraph structure, and vivid personal description. Use advanced vocabulary and complex structures like 'Of all the skills I have acquired', 'Moreover', 'It is a skill that will serve you well'.

content.stem: You have received this email from your English-speaking friend, Chris. Write your email reply. Write 140–190 words.
content.prompt:
From: Chris
Subject: Moving to a new city

Hi,

I've just found out that my family is moving to a completely new city next month. I'm really nervous about it — I'll have to start at a new school and make new friends.

Have you ever had to move or start somewhere new? How did you deal with it? Do you have any advice for me?

I'd really appreciate your help!
Chris
content.sample_answer:
Hi Chris,

I'm sorry to hear you're feeling nervous about the move, but I'm sure things will work out well in the end. I completely understand how you feel, as I went through something similar two years ago.

When my family moved to a different city, I was terrified about starting at a new school. However, I quickly discovered that most people were very welcoming. I made an effort to join clubs and activities, which was the best way to meet people who shared my interests. Within a few weeks, I had already made some good friends.

My advice would be to try to stay positive and be open to new experiences. Don't be afraid to introduce yourself to people — most students are happy to talk to someone new. It might also help to keep in touch with your old friends through video calls, so you don't feel like you've lost those connections entirely.

I'm confident that you'll settle in quickly. Let me know how it goes!

Best wishes

explanation_zh: 中文解析：这是一封回复朋友的邮件（140-190 词）。需要回答三个问题：1）你是否有过类似经历；2）你是如何应对的；3）你有什么建议。语气要友好、鼓励，使用共情表达如 "I completely understand how you feel"。建议部分使用 "My advice would be to..."、"It might also help to..." 等高级句式。
explanation_en: English explanation: This is a reply email (140-190 words) answering three questions: 1) your own experience of moving or starting new; 2) how you dealt with it; 3) your advice. Use a friendly, encouraging tone with empathetic expressions like 'I completely understand how you feel'. For advice, use structures like 'My advice would be to...', 'It might also help to...'.

content.stem: Your teacher has asked you to write a review. Write your review. Write 140–190 words.
content.prompt:
A REVIEW OF A BOOK OR FILM

Your English teacher has asked you to write a review of a book you have read or a film you have seen recently.

In your review, you should:
• briefly describe what happens in the book or film
• say what you particularly liked or disliked about it
• explain whether you would recommend it to other students and why
content.sample_answer:
Review: The Hunger Games by Suzanne Collins

The Hunger Games is set in a dystopian future where young people are forced to fight to the death in a televised competition. The story follows Katniss Everdeen, a brave teenager who volunteers to take her younger sister's place in the Games. Throughout the novel, she must use her intelligence and survival skills to stay alive while questioning the cruel society she lives in.

What I particularly enjoyed about this book was the way Collins creates tension. From the very first page, I found it impossible to put down. The characters are well-developed, and Katniss is a strong, relatable protagonist. I also appreciated the deeper themes about power, inequality, and the media.

If I had to criticise anything, it would be that the ending felt slightly rushed. However, this is a minor issue in an otherwise outstanding novel.

I would wholeheartedly recommend The Hunger Games to other students. It is an exciting, thought-provoking read that appeals to teenagers and adults alike.

explanation_zh: 中文解析：这是一篇书/电影评论（140-190 词）。需要包含三部分：1）简要描述内容/剧情；2）说明你特别喜欢或不喜欢的地方；3）是否推荐给其他同学及原因。使用评论专用词汇如 "protagonist"、"well-developed"、"thought-provoking"。既要有正面评价也可以有小批评，显示批判思维能力。
explanation_en: English explanation: This is a book/film review (140-190 words) with three parts: 1) briefly describe the content/plot; 2) what you liked or disliked; 3) whether you'd recommend it and why. Use review vocabulary like 'protagonist', 'well-developed', 'thought-provoking'. Include both praise and minor criticism to show critical thinking.

content.stem: You see this announcement on an English-language website. Write your article. Write 140–190 words.
content.prompt:
ARTICLES WANTED!

HOW TRAVEL CAN CHANGE YOUR LIFE

Have you ever visited a place that changed the way you think about the world?
Where did you go and what did you experience?
How did this trip change you as a person?

Write us an article answering these questions.
The best articles will be published on our website!
content.sample_answer:
How Travel Can Change Your Life

They say that travel broadens the mind, and I can confirm this from personal experience. A trip I took to a rural village in Yunnan Province two years ago completely transformed my perspective on life.

I had always lived in a busy city, surrounded by technology and convenience. When I arrived at the village, I was struck by how different life was there. The local people lived simply, growing their own food and relying on each other rather than on modern gadgets. Despite having far fewer material possessions than I was used to, they seemed genuinely happy and content.

This experience changed me in several significant ways. First, I stopped taking things for granted and began to appreciate the small pleasures in life. Second, I realised that happiness does not depend on wealth or possessions. Most importantly, I developed a deeper curiosity about different cultures and ways of living.

I would encourage everyone to step outside their comfort zone and explore unfamiliar places. You never know how a journey might change your outlook on life.

explanation_zh: 中文解析：这是一篇杂志文章（140-190 词），讲述旅行如何改变你的人生。要回答三个问题：去了哪里、经历了什么、这次旅行如何改变了你。使用生动的描述和个人反思，展示叙事能力。高级表达如 "transformed my perspective"、"I was struck by"、"step outside their comfort zone" 能提升文章质量。
explanation_en: English explanation: This is a magazine article (140-190 words) about how travel changed your life. Answer three questions: where you went, what you experienced, how the trip changed you. Use vivid description and personal reflection to show narrative ability. Advanced expressions like 'transformed my perspective', 'I was struck by', 'step outside their comfort zone' elevate the article.

content.stem: Your teacher has asked you to write a review. Write your review. Write 140–190 words.
content.prompt:
A REVIEW OF A RESTAURANT OR CAFE

Your English teacher has asked you to write a review of a restaurant or cafe you have visited recently.

In your review, you should:
• describe the place and its atmosphere
• comment on the food and service
• say whether you would recommend it and who it would be suitable for
content.sample_answer:
Review: The Garden Kitchen

The Garden Kitchen is a charming cafe located in the heart of the old town, tucked away down a quiet side street. The moment you step inside, you are greeted by a warm, inviting atmosphere. The interior is decorated with exposed brick walls, wooden furniture, and an abundance of plants, creating a cosy yet stylish environment.

The menu offers a delightful selection of home-made dishes, ranging from hearty soups and freshly baked bread to creative salads and pasta. I ordered the grilled chicken with roasted vegetables, which was beautifully presented and absolutely delicious. The portions were generous, and the prices were remarkably reasonable for the quality on offer. The service was friendly and attentive without being intrusive.

If I had one minor complaint, it would be that the cafe can get quite crowded at weekends, so it is advisable to book a table in advance.

I would highly recommend The Garden Kitchen to anyone looking for a relaxing meal in a beautiful setting. It is particularly suitable for couples and small groups of friends.

explanation_zh: 中文解析：这是一篇餐厅/咖啡馆评论（140-190 词）。需要包含三部分：1）描述地点和氛围；2）评价食物和服务；3）是否推荐以及适合什么人。使用丰富的形容词和感官描写如 "charming"、"inviting"、"beautifully presented"。结尾要给出推荐和适合人群，展示实用的评论写作技巧。
explanation_en: English explanation: This is a restaurant/cafe review (140-190 words) with three parts: 1) describe the place and atmosphere; 2) comment on food and service; 3) whether you'd recommend it and who it suits. Use rich adjectives and sensory descriptions like 'charming', 'inviting', 'beautifully presented'. End with a recommendation and target audience to show practical review-writing skills.

content.stem: Your school principal has asked students to write a report. Write your report. Write 140–190 words.
content.prompt:
REPORT ON AFTER-SCHOOL ACTIVITIES

The school principal wants to improve the range of after-school activities available to students. You have been asked to write a report.

In your report, you should:
• describe the current after-school activities
• explain what students think about them
• suggest improvements or new activities that could be introduced
content.sample_answer:
Report on After-School Activities

Introduction
The purpose of this report is to evaluate the current after-school activities at our school and to suggest improvements based on student feedback.

Current Situation
At present, the school offers a limited range of after-school activities, including basketball, football, and a reading club. While these are popular with some students, many feel that the options are too narrow and do not cater to a wide variety of interests.

Student Feedback
A survey of 150 students revealed that 65% would like more creative activities, such as drama, photography, and music production. Several students also expressed interest in technology-related clubs, including coding and robotics. Additionally, many students mentioned that the current activities are only available twice a week, which they find insufficient.

Recommendations
Based on these findings, I would recommend the following:
• Introduce at least three new clubs focusing on creative and technological activities.
• Increase the frequency of sessions to three or four times per week.
• Invite external instructors to lead specialist workshops.

I believe these changes would significantly increase student participation and satisfaction.

explanation_zh: 中文解析：这是一篇正式报告（140-190 词）。报告需要有清晰的结构，通常包含：引言（说明目的）、现状描述、反馈/调查结果、建议/推荐。使用正式语言和客观的语气，避免过度个人化。报告格式要求使用小标题来组织内容，建议部分用要点符号列出。
explanation_en: English explanation: This is a formal report (140-190 words). Reports need clear structure: introduction (state purpose), current situation, feedback/findings, recommendations. Use formal, objective language — avoid being overly personal. Report format requires subheadings to organise content, and bullet points for recommendations.

content.stem: Your school principal has asked students to write a report. Write your report. Write 140–190 words.
content.prompt:
REPORT ON SCHOOL FACILITIES

The school principal is considering upgrading some of the school facilities and has asked students to write a report.

In your report, you should:
• describe which facilities are most in need of improvement
• explain how students currently feel about these facilities
• make recommendations for specific upgrades
content.sample_answer:
Report on School Facilities

Introduction
This report aims to identify the school facilities most in need of improvement and to propose specific upgrades based on student opinions.

Facilities Requiring Improvement
The two areas that students feel most strongly about are the library and the sports facilities. The library, although well-stocked, has an outdated computer section with slow machines and limited seating. The sports hall lacks modern equipment, and the changing rooms are in poor condition.

Student Opinions
According to a recent questionnaire, 78% of students rated the library computers as 'poor' or 'very poor.' Meanwhile, 72% expressed dissatisfaction with the sports facilities, particularly the lack of an indoor swimming pool and the worn-out athletics track.

Recommendations
I would suggest the following improvements:
• Replace the library computers with up-to-date models and install additional power sockets for students' laptops.
• Refurbish the changing rooms and invest in new gym equipment.
• Consider a long-term plan to build a small indoor pool.

Implementing these upgrades would greatly enhance both the academic and physical wellbeing of students.

explanation_zh: 中文解析：这是另一篇正式报告（140-190 词），讨论学校设施改善。同样需要清晰的小标题结构：引言、需要改善的设施、学生意见、建议。使用数据和调查结果来支持论点（可以编造合理的数据）。正式表达如 "This report aims to"、"According to a recent questionnaire"、"I would suggest the following" 体现报告的专业性。
explanation_en: English explanation: This is another formal report (140-190 words) about school facility improvements. Use clear subheading structure: introduction, facilities needing improvement, student opinions, recommendations. Support arguments with data and survey results (you may invent reasonable figures). Formal expressions like 'This report aims to', 'According to a recent questionnaire', 'I would suggest the following' convey professionalism.

content.stem: You have received this letter from the director of a language school. Write your letter of application. Write 140–190 words.
content.prompt:
Dear Applicant,

We are looking for enthusiastic young people to work as summer camp assistants at our international language school. Assistants will help organise activities, support teachers, and look after students aged 10–14 from different countries.

Please write to us explaining:
• why you are interested in this position
• what relevant experience or skills you have
• when you would be available to work

We look forward to hearing from you.

Ms Rachel Thompson
Director, Sunshine Language School
content.sample_answer:
Dear Ms Thompson,

I am writing to apply for the position of summer camp assistant at Sunshine Language School. I believe I would be an excellent candidate for this role, and I am very enthusiastic about the opportunity.

I am particularly interested in this position because I am passionate about working with young people and I have always enjoyed multicultural environments. As someone who speaks both Chinese and English fluently, I understand the challenges of learning a new language and would be able to empathise with students from diverse backgrounds.

Regarding my experience, I have volunteered as a teaching assistant at my local primary school for the past year, where I helped organise classroom activities and supported children with their reading. I also have considerable experience in team sports, which has taught me valuable leadership and communication skills.

I would be available to work throughout July and August, as my school holidays begin in late June. I am flexible with hours and happy to work at weekends if necessary.

I would welcome the opportunity to discuss my application further. Thank you for considering me.

Yours sincerely

explanation_zh: 中文解析：这是一封正式求职信（140-190 词）。需要回答三个问题：为什么对这个职位感兴趣、你有什么相关经验或技能、何时可以工作。使用正式信件格式："Dear Ms Thompson" 开头，"Yours sincerely" 结尾。正式表达如 "I am writing to apply for"、"I would welcome the opportunity to" 是必须的。语气要专业、自信但谦逊。
explanation_en: English explanation: This is a formal letter of application (140-190 words). Answer three questions: why you're interested, your relevant experience/skills, your availability. Use formal letter format: 'Dear Ms Thompson' opening, 'Yours sincerely' closing. Formal expressions like 'I am writing to apply for', 'I would welcome the opportunity to' are essential. Tone should be professional, confident but modest.

content.stem: You see this announcement on an English-language website. Write your article. Write 140–190 words.
content.prompt:
ARTICLES WANTED!

IS IT BETTER TO LEARN FROM EXPERIENCE OR FROM BOOKS?

Some people believe the best way to learn is through real-life experience, while others prefer learning from books and formal education.

What is your view? Give examples from your own life to support your answer.

The best articles will be published next month!
content.sample_answer:
Is It Better to Learn from Experience or from Books?

This is a question that has fascinated thinkers for centuries, and I believe the answer lies in combining both approaches. However, if I had to choose one, I would say that experience is the more powerful teacher.

Books and formal education provide us with essential theoretical knowledge. Without them, we would have no foundation upon which to build our understanding of the world. For instance, I could never have learned mathematics or history without textbooks and dedicated teachers.

However, some of the most valuable lessons I have learned came through direct experience. When I started my first part-time job at a bookshop, I quickly developed skills that no classroom could have taught me — dealing with difficult customers, managing my time, and working under pressure. These real-world challenges forced me to adapt and grow in ways that reading alone could never achieve.

Ultimately, I believe that the ideal approach is to use books as a starting point and then reinforce that knowledge through practical experience. Together, they form a complete education.

explanation_zh: 中文解析：这是一篇杂志文章（140-190 词），讨论从经验中学习还是从书本中学习更好。需要表达你的观点并用个人经历来支持。文章需要有引人入胜的开头和有力的结尾。使用对比结构（Books provide... However, experience...）和个人案例来增强说服力。高级表达如 "if I had to choose"、"no classroom could have taught me" 展示语言水平。
explanation_en: English explanation: This is a magazine article (140-190 words) discussing whether learning from experience or books is better. Express your view with personal examples. The article needs an engaging opening and strong conclusion. Use comparison structures (Books provide... However, experience...) and personal anecdotes for persuasiveness. Advanced expressions like 'if I had to choose', 'no classroom could have taught me' demonstrate language level.

content.stem: Your teacher has asked you to write a review. Write your review. Write 140–190 words.
content.prompt:
A REVIEW OF A TV SERIES

Your English teacher has asked you to write a review of a TV series you have watched recently.

In your review, you should:
• briefly describe what the series is about
• say what makes it particularly enjoyable or disappointing
• explain whether you would recommend it and to what kind of viewer
content.sample_answer:
Review: Sherlock (BBC)

Sherlock is a modern adaptation of Arthur Conan Doyle's classic detective stories, set in present-day London. The series follows Sherlock Holmes, a brilliant but socially awkward consulting detective, and his loyal companion Dr John Watson as they solve a variety of complex and often dangerous cases.

What makes this series particularly enjoyable is the exceptional quality of the writing and acting. Benedict Cumberbatch brings an electrifying energy to the role of Sherlock, while Martin Freeman provides the perfect counterbalance as the more grounded Watson. The plots are ingeniously constructed, with unexpected twists that keep you guessing until the very end. The cinematography is also outstanding, capturing London in a way that feels both familiar and mysterious.

If I had one criticism, it would be the long wait between series — sometimes two or three years — which can be frustrating for devoted fans.

I would wholeheartedly recommend Sherlock to anyone who enjoys crime dramas with intelligent storylines. It is equally suitable for teenagers and adults who appreciate sharp dialogue and compelling characters.

explanation_zh: 中文解析：这是一篇电视剧评论（140-190 词）。需要包含三部分：1）简要介绍剧集内容；2）说明为什么特别好看或令人失望；3）是否推荐以及适合什么类型的观众。使用影视评论专用词汇如 "adaptation"、"cinematography"、"compelling characters"。结构要有正面评价也有小批评，最后给出推荐。
explanation_en: English explanation: This is a TV series review (140-190 words) with three parts: 1) briefly describe the series; 2) explain what makes it enjoyable or disappointing; 3) whether you'd recommend it and to whom. Use TV/film review vocabulary like 'adaptation', 'cinematography', 'compelling characters'. Structure with both praise and minor criticism, ending with a recommendation.

content.stem: You see this announcement on an English-language website. Write your article. Write 140–190 words.
content.prompt:
ARTICLES WANTED!

A PERSON WHO HAS INFLUENCED YOU

Who is the person who has had the greatest influence on your life?
How have they influenced you?
What have you learned from them?

Write us an article answering these questions.
The best articles will be published on our website!
content.sample_answer:
A Person Who Has Influenced Me

When I think about the people who have shaped me as a person, one individual stands out above all others: my secondary school English teacher, Ms Wang.

Ms Wang was not just an exceptional teacher — she was someone who genuinely believed in every student's potential. When I first entered her class, I was shy and lacked confidence in my English ability. She noticed this immediately and went out of her way to encourage me, always offering constructive feedback rather than criticism.

Her influence on me has been profound. She taught me that making mistakes is an essential part of learning and that perseverance is more important than natural talent. More importantly, she showed me the power of believing in yourself. Thanks to her encouragement, I began to participate more actively in class, entered English competitions, and eventually developed a genuine love for the language.

The lesson I have taken from Ms Wang is that a single person's kindness and patience can transform someone's life. I hope one day to have the same positive impact on others.

explanation_zh: 中文解析：这是一篇关于影响你的人的杂志文章（140-190 词）。回答三个问题：谁对你影响最大、他们如何影响了你、你从他们身上学到了什么。使用具体的故事细节和个人反思。高级表达如 "stands out above all others"、"went out of her way"、"profound" 展示叙事和表达能力。
explanation_en: English explanation: This is a magazine article about a person who has influenced you (140-190 words). Answer three questions: who has influenced you most, how they influenced you, what you learned from them. Use specific story details and personal reflection. Advanced expressions like 'stands out above all others', 'went out of her way', 'profound' demonstrate narrative and expressive ability.

content.stem: You have received this email from your English-speaking friend, Sarah. Write your email reply. Write 140–190 words.
content.prompt:
From: Sarah
Subject: Career ideas

Hi,

I'm starting to think about what career I want to pursue after university, but I have no idea what to do! I'm interested in so many things that I find it impossible to choose.

How did you decide what you want to do in the future? What do you think is the most important factor when choosing a career — money, enjoyment, or something else?

I'd love to hear your thoughts!
Sarah
content.sample_answer:
Hi Sarah,

It's completely normal to feel overwhelmed when thinking about your future career — I went through exactly the same thing not long ago! Let me share how I approached the decision.

For me, the turning point came when I stopped thinking about specific job titles and started focusing on what activities genuinely energise me. I realised that I love solving problems and working with people, which led me to consider careers in areas like project management and education. I found it helpful to do some voluntary work and job shadowing to get a real sense of what different roles involve.

As for what matters most when choosing a career, I firmly believe that enjoyment should come first. If you spend every day doing something you find meaningful, you are far more likely to excel at it — and the financial rewards often follow naturally. That said, it is also practical to consider whether a career can provide the lifestyle you want.

My advice would be to explore as much as possible before making a decision. There's no rush — the right path will become clearer with time.

Take care!

explanation_zh: 中文解析：这是一封回复朋友关于职业选择的邮件（140-190 词）。需要回答两个问题：你是怎么决定未来方向的、选择职业时最重要的因素是什么。语气要友好支持，分享个人经验并给出建议。高级表达如 "the turning point came when"、"I firmly believe that" 展示语言水平。
explanation_en: English explanation: This is a reply email about career choices (140-190 words). Answer two questions: how you decided your future direction, what the most important factor is when choosing a career. Use a friendly, supportive tone, share personal experience, and give advice. Advanced expressions like 'the turning point came when', 'I firmly believe that' demonstrate language level.

content.stem: Your teacher has asked you to write a review. Write your review. Write 140–190 words.
content.prompt:
A REVIEW OF AN APP OR WEBSITE

Your English teacher has asked you to write a review of an app or website that you use regularly.

In your review, you should:
• explain what the app or website is for and how it works
• describe what you find most useful and any drawbacks
• say whether you would recommend it to other students
content.sample_answer:
Review: Duolingo Language Learning App

Duolingo is a free language-learning app that uses gamification to make studying a new language fun and engaging. The app offers courses in over forty languages and is available on both smartphones and computers.

The way it works is simple: users complete short lessons covering vocabulary, grammar, and listening comprehension. Each lesson takes only five to ten minutes, making it easy to fit into a busy schedule. The app tracks your progress with streaks and achievements, which provides excellent motivation to practise daily.

What I find most useful is the variety of exercises and the instant feedback on mistakes. The app also adapts to your level, presenting more challenging material as you improve. However, one significant drawback is that it focuses heavily on translation and basic phrases rather than developing genuine conversational ability. It is best used as a supplement to other learning methods.

I would definitely recommend Duolingo to other students, particularly those who want a convenient and enjoyable way to build vocabulary and reinforce grammar in a new language.

explanation_zh: 中文解析：这是一篇应用/网站评论（140-190 词）。需要包含三部分：1）解释这个应用/网站的用途和使用方式；2）最有用的功能和不足之处；3）是否推荐给其他同学。使用科技相关词汇如 "gamification"、"track your progress"。展示你能平衡评价正面和负面方面。
explanation_en: English explanation: This is an app/website review (140-190 words) with three parts: 1) explain what it's for and how it works; 2) most useful features and drawbacks; 3) whether you'd recommend it. Use tech-related vocabulary like 'gamification', 'track your progress'. Show you can balance positive and negative evaluation.

content.stem: You have received this letter from the manager of a local charity. Write your letter of application. Write 140–190 words.
content.prompt:
Dear Volunteer,

Our charity is looking for young volunteers to help at a community centre during the winter holidays. Volunteers will assist with organising events for elderly residents, helping with meal preparation, and entertaining children.

Please write to us explaining:
• why you would like to volunteer with us
• what skills or qualities you could bring to the role
• which of the above tasks you would be most interested in and why

We look forward to your response.

Mr James Cooper
Manager, Greenwood Community Centre
content.sample_answer:
Dear Mr Cooper,

I am writing to express my interest in volunteering at Greenwood Community Centre during the winter holidays. I believe this would be a wonderful opportunity to give something back to the community while gaining valuable experience.

I would like to volunteer because I am passionate about helping others and I feel it is important for young people to support their local community. Having been raised by my grandparents, I have a particular affinity for working with elderly people and understand the importance of companionship, especially during the holidays when loneliness can be most keenly felt.

In terms of skills, I am patient, responsible, and an excellent communicator. I also have experience organising events through my school's student council, where I helped plan charity fundraisers and social gatherings.

Of the tasks listed, I would be most interested in organising events for elderly residents. I believe I could create enjoyable activities such as quiz evenings, craft sessions, and music afternoons that would bring genuine pleasure to participants.

Thank you for considering my application. I would be delighted to discuss it further at your convenience.

Yours sincerely

explanation_zh: 中文解析：这是一封正式的志愿者申请信（140-190 词）。需要回答三个问题：为什么想做志愿者、你能带来什么技能或品质、你最感兴趣的任务。使用正式信件格式和表达。展示你的热情和与职位相关的个人经历。高级表达如 "give something back"、"particular affinity"、"keenly felt" 展示语言能力。
explanation_en: English explanation: This is a formal volunteer application letter (140-190 words). Answer three questions: why you want to volunteer, what skills/qualities you bring, which task interests you most. Use formal letter format and expressions. Show enthusiasm and relevant personal experience. Advanced expressions like 'give something back', 'particular affinity', 'keenly felt' demonstrate language ability.

content.stem: You see this announcement on an English-language website. Write your article. Write 140–190 words.
content.prompt:
ARTICLES WANTED!

THE IMPORTANCE OF HAVING A HOBBY

Do you have a hobby that is important to you?
What do you enjoy about it?
How has it benefited you personally?

Write us an article answering these questions.
The best articles will be published next month!
content.sample_answer:
The Importance of Having a Hobby

In today's busy world, it is all too easy to become consumed by work and responsibilities. That is precisely why having a hobby is so important — and for me, that hobby is photography.

I took up photography three years ago after receiving a camera as a birthday present. At first, I simply enjoyed taking pictures of landscapes and street scenes. However, as my skills developed, I began to see the world around me with entirely new eyes. Photography taught me to notice beauty in unexpected places — a pattern of shadows on a wall, the way light falls on water, or the expression on a stranger's face.

The benefits have been considerable. Photography has given me a creative outlet that provides a welcome escape from academic pressure. It has improved my patience and attention to detail, and it has connected me with a community of like-minded people through online forums and local photography clubs.

I would encourage everyone to find a hobby they are passionate about. It need not be expensive or time-consuming — what matters is that it brings you joy and a sense of fulfilment.

explanation_zh: 中文解析：这是一篇关于爱好重要性的杂志文章（140-190 词）。回答三个问题：你的爱好是什么、你喜欢它什么、它在个人层面给你带来了什么好处。使用生动的描述和个人反思。高级表达如 "consumed by"、"with entirely new eyes"、"creative outlet" 展示语言水平。
explanation_en: English explanation: This is a magazine article about the importance of hobbies (140-190 words). Answer three questions: what your hobby is, what you enjoy about it, how it has benefited you. Use vivid description and personal reflection. Advanced expressions like 'consumed by', 'with entirely new eyes', 'creative outlet' demonstrate language level.

content.stem: Your school principal has asked students to write a report. Write your report. Write 140–190 words.
content.prompt:
REPORT ON STUDENT WELLBEING

The school principal is concerned about student stress levels and wants to introduce measures to improve student wellbeing. You have been asked to write a report.

In your report, you should:
• identify the main causes of stress among students
• describe how stress affects students' performance and health
• recommend specific measures the school could take
content.sample_answer:
Report on Student Wellbeing

Introduction
This report examines the main causes of stress among students at our school and proposes measures to improve overall wellbeing.

Causes of Stress
Based on informal discussions with students across all year groups, the primary sources of stress are excessive homework, exam pressure, and a lack of time for relaxation and social activities. Many students also reported feeling anxious about university entrance requirements and future career prospects.

Impact on Performance and Health
Stress has a noticeable impact on both academic performance and health. Students who feel overwhelmed tend to produce lower-quality work and are more likely to be absent from school. Common physical symptoms include headaches, difficulty sleeping, and loss of appetite. Several students mentioned experiencing anxiety and low mood.

Recommendations
To address these issues, I would recommend the following:
• Introduce a homework-free evening each week to allow students to rest.
• Provide access to a trained school counsellor for confidential support.
• Create a dedicated 'wellbeing room' where students can relax during break times.
• Organise regular mindfulness and stress-management workshops.

These measures would demonstrate the school's commitment to supporting students beyond their academic needs.

explanation_zh: 中文解析：这是一篇关于学生福祉的正式报告（140-190 词）。使用清晰的小标题结构：引言、压力原因、对表现和健康的影响、建议。使用正式、客观的语言，引用调查结果或学生反馈。建议要具体可行。高级表达如 "based on informal discussions"、"noticeable impact"、"demonstrate the school's commitment" 体现报告专业性。
explanation_en: English explanation: This is a formal report on student wellbeing (140-190 words). Use clear subheading structure: introduction, causes of stress, impact on performance and health, recommendations. Use formal, objective language, citing survey results or student feedback. Recommendations should be specific and actionable. Advanced expressions like 'based on informal discussions', 'noticeable impact', 'demonstrate the school's commitment' convey professionalism.

content.stem: You have received this email from your English-speaking friend, Alex. Write your email reply. Write 140–190 words.
content.prompt:
From: Alex
Subject: Healthy eating

Hi,

I've been trying to eat more healthily lately, but I'm finding it really difficult. I love junk food and I don't know how to cook anything healthy that actually tastes good!

Do you eat healthily? What healthy foods do you enjoy? Do you have any tips for someone trying to change their eating habits?

Thanks!
Alex
content.sample_answer:
Hi Alex,

It's great to hear that you're trying to eat more healthily — that's a really positive step! I completely understand the struggle, as I went through something very similar last year.

I wouldn't say I eat perfectly, but I've made some changes that have really helped. I try to cook most of my meals at home using fresh ingredients, and I've discovered that healthy food can actually be delicious. For example, I love making stir-fries with lots of vegetables and a tasty sauce — they're quick, easy, and full of flavour. I'm also a big fan of overnight oats for breakfast, which take just five minutes to prepare the night before.

My biggest tip would be to make small changes gradually rather than trying to overhaul your diet overnight. Start by replacing one unhealthy snack with a piece of fruit or some nuts. Another tip is to find healthy recipes online and experiment in the kitchen — you might surprise yourself! It also helps to prepare meals in advance, so you're less tempted by fast food when you're hungry and tired.

Good luck with it!

Best wishes

explanation_zh: 中文解析：这是一封回复朋友关于健康饮食的邮件（140-190 词）。需要回答三个问题：你是否吃得健康、你喜欢什么健康食物、给想改变饮食习惯的人什么建议。语气要友好鼓励，分享个人经验。高级表达如 "overhaul your diet overnight"、"you might surprise yourself" 展示语言能力。
explanation_en: English explanation: This is a reply email about healthy eating (140-190 words). Answer three questions: do you eat healthily, what healthy foods you enjoy, tips for changing eating habits. Use a friendly, encouraging tone and share personal experience. Advanced expressions like 'overhaul your diet overnight', 'you might surprise yourself' demonstrate language ability.

content.stem: Your teacher has asked you to write a review. Write your review. Write 140–190 words.
content.prompt:
A REVIEW OF A MUSEUM OR EXHIBITION

Your English teacher has asked you to write a review of a museum or exhibition you have visited recently.

In your review, you should:
• describe what the museum or exhibition is about and what you saw
• say what you found most interesting or impressive
• explain whether you would recommend a visit and why
content.sample_answer:
Review: The National Science Museum — 'Exploring the Universe' Exhibition

The 'Exploring the Universe' exhibition at the National Science Museum takes visitors on a fascinating journey through space, from our own solar system to the most distant galaxies. The exhibition features interactive displays, life-sized models of spacecraft, and stunning images captured by the latest space telescopes.

What I found most impressive was the virtual reality section, where visitors can experience what it would be like to walk on the surface of Mars. The sensation of low gravity and the panoramic view of the red landscape were breathtakingly realistic. I also particularly enjoyed the timeline of space exploration, which traces humanity's journey from early astronomy to modern-day missions in an engaging and accessible way.

If I had one minor complaint, it would be that some sections were quite crowded, which made it difficult to spend as much time at certain displays as I would have liked.

I would strongly recommend this exhibition to anyone with an interest in science and space. It is suitable for all ages and provides a truly unforgettable experience.

explanation_zh: 中文解析：这是一篇博物馆/展览评论（140-190 词）。需要包含三部分：1）描述博物馆/展览的主题和你看到了什么；2）你觉得最有趣或印象最深的地方；3）是否推荐参观及原因。使用描述性语言和感官细节。高级表达如 "breathtakingly realistic"、"traces humanity's journey"、"truly unforgettable" 提升文章质量。
explanation_en: English explanation: This is a museum/exhibition review (140-190 words) with three parts: 1) describe the theme and what you saw; 2) what you found most interesting or impressive; 3) whether you'd recommend a visit and why. Use descriptive language and sensory details. Advanced expressions like 'breathtakingly realistic', 'traces humanity's journey', 'truly unforgettable' elevate the article.

content.stem: You see this announcement on an English-language website. Write your article. Write 140–190 words.
content.prompt:
ARTICLES WANTED!

THE DIGITAL DETOX — COULD YOU LIVE WITHOUT YOUR PHONE?

Have you ever tried going without your smartphone for a period of time?
What was the experience like?
Would you recommend it to others?

Write us an article answering these questions.
The best articles will be published on our website!
content.sample_answer:
The Digital Detox — Could You Live Without Your Phone?

Like most teenagers, I used to check my phone obsessively — first thing in the morning, during meals, and even while studying. So when my school organised a 'digital detox week' last term, I was genuinely terrified at the prospect of being without my smartphone for seven whole days.

The first two days were undeniably difficult. I kept reaching for my phone out of habit and felt oddly disconnected from the world. However, by the third day, something remarkable happened. I began to notice things I had been ignoring — the quality of conversations improved, I slept better, and I had significantly more time for activities I had been neglecting, such as reading and exercising.

By the end of the week, I felt calmer, more focused, and more present in my daily life. The experience made me realise how much of my time and attention was being consumed by endless scrolling.

I would wholeheartedly recommend a digital detox to anyone who feels overwhelmed by technology. You do not need to give up your phone permanently — even a weekend without it can be genuinely transformative.

explanation_zh: 中文解析：这是一篇关于数字排毒的杂志文章（140-190 词）。回答三个问题：你是否尝试过不用手机、体验如何、是否推荐。使用叙事技巧描述体验的变化过程（从困难到积极转变）。高级表达如 "oddly disconnected"、"something remarkable happened"、"genuinely transformative" 展示语言水平。
explanation_en: English explanation: This is a magazine article about digital detox (140-190 words). Answer three questions: have you tried going without your phone, what was the experience like, would you recommend it. Use narrative techniques to describe the transformation (from difficulty to positive change). Advanced expressions like 'oddly disconnected', 'something remarkable happened', 'genuinely transformative' demonstrate language level.

content.stem: You have received this letter from the coordinator of an international student exchange programme. Write your letter of application. Write 140–190 words.
content.prompt:
Dear Applicant,

We are offering places on a two-week cultural exchange programme in the United Kingdom this summer. Successful applicants will stay with a British host family, attend local schools, and participate in cultural activities.

Please write to us explaining:
• why you would like to participate in this programme
• how this experience would benefit you personally
• what you could contribute to the programme

We look forward to receiving your application.

Dr Emily Hughes
Coordinator, Global Youth Exchange Programme
content.sample_answer:
Dear Dr Hughes,

I am writing to apply for a place on the Global Youth Exchange Programme in the United Kingdom this summer. I believe this would be an extraordinary opportunity for personal and academic growth.

I would like to participate because I have always been fascinated by British culture, history, and education. Experiencing daily life in the UK would give me a far deeper understanding of the country than any textbook could provide. Living with a host family would also offer an invaluable opportunity to practise my English in authentic, everyday situations.

This experience would benefit me enormously. As someone hoping to study abroad in the future, spending two weeks immersed in British culture would help me develop the independence and cross-cultural awareness necessary for living in a foreign country.

In terms of what I could contribute, I would be eager to share my own Chinese culture with my host family and fellow participants. I could give presentations about Chinese festivals, teach basic Mandarin, and prepare traditional Chinese dishes.

I would be extremely grateful for the opportunity to take part. Thank you for considering my application.

Yours sincerely

explanation_zh: 中文解析：这是一封申请参加国际交换项目的正式信（140-190 词）。回答三个问题：为什么想参加、这个经历如何在个人层面使你受益、你能为项目贡献什么。展示热情和具体的贡献想法。高级表达如 "extraordinary opportunity"、"immersed in British culture"、"cross-cultural awareness" 展示语言水平。
explanation_en: English explanation: This is a formal application letter for an international exchange programme (140-190 words). Answer three questions: why you want to participate, how the experience would benefit you, what you could contribute. Show enthusiasm and specific contribution ideas. Advanced expressions like 'extraordinary opportunity', 'immersed in British culture', 'cross-cultural awareness' demonstrate language level.

content.stem: Your teacher has asked you to write a review. Write your review. Write 140–190 words.
content.prompt:
A REVIEW OF A PODCAST OR YOUTUBE CHANNEL

Your English teacher has asked you to write a review of a podcast or YouTube channel you follow regularly.

In your review, you should:
• explain what the podcast or channel is about
• say what makes it stand out from similar content
• explain who would enjoy it and whether you would recommend it
content.sample_answer:
Review: 'TED Talks Daily' Podcast

TED Talks Daily is a podcast that features a different TED Talk every day, covering an extraordinarily wide range of topics — from artificial intelligence and climate science to psychology, art, and personal development. Each episode runs for approximately fifteen to twenty minutes, making it perfect for listening during a commute or study break.

What makes this podcast stand out from similar content is the consistently high quality of its speakers and ideas. Every talk is delivered by an expert in their field, and the content is designed to be both informative and thought-provoking. Unlike many educational podcasts, TED Talks Daily manages to make complex subjects accessible without oversimplifying them. The production quality is also excellent, with clear audio and helpful introductions to each episode.

One minor drawback is that the topics vary so widely that not every episode will appeal to every listener. However, this variety is also one of its greatest strengths.

I would strongly recommend this podcast to any student who is curious about the world and wants to expand their knowledge in an enjoyable and convenient way.

explanation_zh: 中文解析：这是一篇播客/YouTube频道评论（140-190 词）。需要包含三部分：1）解释播客/频道的内容；2）它的突出特点；3）谁会喜欢它以及是否推荐。使用媒体评论词汇如 "production quality"、"thought-provoking"、"accessible"。展示你能评价数字内容的能力。
explanation_en: English explanation: This is a podcast/YouTube channel review (140-190 words) with three parts: 1) explain what it's about; 2) what makes it stand out; 3) who would enjoy it and whether you'd recommend it. Use media review vocabulary like 'production quality', 'thought-provoking', 'accessible'. Show you can evaluate digital content.

content.stem: Your school principal has asked students to write a report. Write your report. Write 140–190 words.
content.prompt:
REPORT ON ENVIRONMENTAL PRACTICES AT SCHOOL

The school principal wants to make the school more environmentally friendly and has asked students to write a report.

In your report, you should:
• describe the school's current environmental practices
• identify areas where the school could improve
• suggest specific actions to make the school greener
content.sample_answer:
Report on Environmental Practices at School

Introduction
The purpose of this report is to assess the school's current environmental practices and to recommend ways to make our school more sustainable.

Current Practices
At present, the school has recycling bins in most classrooms and uses energy-efficient lighting in the main building. However, beyond these basic measures, there is relatively little emphasis on environmental responsibility. Paper consumption remains very high, and single-use plastic bottles are widely used by both staff and students.

Areas for Improvement
The most pressing issues are excessive paper usage, a lack of composting facilities, and limited awareness among students about sustainable practices. The canteen also produces a considerable amount of food waste, much of which ends up in general rubbish bins.

Recommendations
I would propose the following actions:
• Encourage digital submission of assignments to reduce paper usage.
• Install water-refill stations and ban single-use plastic bottles on campus.
• Introduce a composting programme for food waste from the canteen.
• Establish a student-led 'Green Team' to promote environmental awareness through campaigns and events.

These initiatives would significantly reduce the school's environmental footprint while educating students about sustainability.

explanation_zh: 中文解析：这是一篇关于学校环保实践的正式报告（140-190 词）。使用清晰的小标题结构：引言、现有做法、改进空间、建议。使用正式客观的语言和具体可行的建议。高级表达如 "assess"、"sustainable practices"、"environmental footprint" 体现报告专业性。
explanation_en: English explanation: This is a formal report on school environmental practices (140-190 words). Use clear subheading structure: introduction, current practices, areas for improvement, recommendations. Use formal, objective language with specific, actionable suggestions. Advanced expressions like 'assess', 'sustainable practices', 'environmental footprint' convey professionalism.

content.stem: You see this announcement on an English-language website. Write your article. Write 140–190 words.
content.prompt:
ARTICLES WANTED!

WHAT MAKES A GOOD LEADER?

What qualities do you think a good leader should have?
Can you think of someone you know or admire who demonstrates these qualities?
Why do you think good leadership matters in today's world?

Write us an article answering these questions.
The best articles will be published on our website!
content.sample_answer:
What Makes a Good Leader?

Leadership is a quality that is often discussed but rarely defined clearly. In my view, the most essential qualities of a good leader are integrity, empathy, and the ability to inspire others.

A leader with integrity earns the trust and respect of those around them. People are far more willing to follow someone they believe is honest and acts in the best interests of the group. Empathy is equally important, as a good leader must understand the perspectives and concerns of the people they lead. Finally, the ability to inspire — to paint a vision of a better future and motivate others to work towards it — is what distinguishes a truly exceptional leader from an ordinary one.

Someone who embodies these qualities is my school headteacher, who always listens to students' ideas and leads by example rather than by authority. Her calm, thoughtful approach has transformed the culture of our school.

In today's complex world, good leadership matters more than ever. From business to politics to community life, effective leaders have the power to bring people together and drive positive change.

explanation_zh: 中文解析：这是一篇关于优秀领导者品质的杂志文章（140-190 词）。回答三个问题：好的领导者应该有什么品质、你认识或崇拜的谁体现了这些品质、为什么好的领导力在当今世界很重要。使用具体例子和抽象分析相结合。高级表达如 "earns the trust"、"distinguishes"、"leads by example" 展示语言水平。
explanation_en: English explanation: This is a magazine article about leadership qualities (140-190 words). Answer three questions: what qualities a good leader should have, someone who demonstrates these qualities, why good leadership matters today. Combine specific examples with abstract analysis. Advanced expressions like 'earns the trust', 'distinguishes', 'leads by example' demonstrate language level.

content.stem: You have received this email from your English-speaking friend, Tom. Write your email reply. Write 140–190 words.
content.prompt:
From: Tom
Subject: Learning a new language

Hi,

I've decided to start learning Mandarin Chinese! I know you speak it as your first language, so I was hoping you could give me some advice.

What do you think is the hardest part about learning Chinese? What's the best way to get started? Are there any resources you would recommend?

I'm really excited about this!
Tom
content.sample_answer:
Hi Tom,

How exciting that you've decided to learn Mandarin! I'd be happy to share some advice based on my own experience as a native speaker and as someone who has helped friends learn the language.

Honestly, the hardest part about learning Chinese is probably the tones. Mandarin has four tones, and the same syllable can mean completely different things depending on the tone you use. This can be confusing at first, but with regular listening and speaking practice, it becomes much more natural over time. The characters can also seem overwhelming initially, but learning the basic radicals helps you understand how they are structured.

The best way to get started is to focus on spoken Chinese first — learn common phrases, practise pronunciation, and listen to as much Mandarin as possible. I would recommend the apps Pleco and HelloChinese, which are both excellent for beginners. Watching Chinese TV shows with subtitles is also a fantastic way to improve your listening skills.

I'd be more than happy to practise speaking with you whenever you like!

Good luck!

explanation_zh: 中文解析：这是一封给想学中文的外国朋友的建议邮件（140-190 词）。回答三个问题：学中文最难的部分、最好的入门方法、推荐的资源。语气要友好鼓励，提供具体实用的建议。利用你作为母语者的独特视角。高级表达如 "based on my own experience"、"becomes much more natural" 展示语言水平。
explanation_en: English explanation: This is an advice email to a friend wanting to learn Chinese (140-190 words). Answer three questions: hardest part of learning Chinese, best way to get started, recommended resources. Use a friendly, encouraging tone with specific practical advice. Leverage your unique perspective as a native speaker. Advanced expressions like 'based on my own experience', 'becomes much more natural' demonstrate language level.

content.stem: Your teacher has asked you to write a review. Write your review. Write 140–190 words.
content.prompt:
A REVIEW OF A LIVE EVENT

Your English teacher has asked you to write a review of a live event you have attended, such as a concert, sports match, festival, or theatre performance.

In your review, you should:
• describe the event and the venue
• say what you enjoyed most and what could have been better
• explain whether you would recommend this type of event to others
content.sample_answer:
Review: The Annual City Music Festival

Last month, I attended the Annual City Music Festival, a two-day outdoor event held in Central Park that features a diverse line-up of local and international musicians. The venue was spectacularly set up, with a main stage surrounded by food stalls, art installations, and comfortable seating areas spread across the park.

What I enjoyed most was the incredible variety of music on offer. Over the two days, I heard everything from jazz and folk to electronic and hip-hop, and every act brought something unique to the stage. The highlight for me was a young singer-songwriter whose powerful voice and heartfelt lyrics moved the entire audience to tears. The atmosphere was electric, with thousands of people singing, dancing, and sharing the experience together.

If I had to suggest an improvement, the queues for food were excessively long at peak times, which meant missing parts of some performances. Better organisation of refreshments would have enhanced the experience considerably.

I would enthusiastically recommend this festival to anyone who loves live music. It is an unforgettable celebration of creativity and community that deserves a much wider audience.

explanation_zh: 中文解析：这是一篇现场活动评论（140-190 词）。需要包含三部分：1）描述活动和场地；2）最喜欢的部分和可以改进的地方；3）是否推荐。使用生动的描述语言和感官细节，如 "electric atmosphere"、"heartfelt lyrics"。展示你能对现场体验进行全面评价的能力。
explanation_en: English explanation: This is a live event review (140-190 words) with three parts: 1) describe the event and venue; 2) what you enjoyed most and what could be better; 3) whether you'd recommend it. Use vivid descriptive language and sensory details like 'electric atmosphere', 'heartfelt lyrics'. Show you can comprehensively evaluate a live experience.
